children.text: A court shall issue an order enforcing this subchapter under

children.tail: only if the court finds that—

children.text: alternative technologies or capabilities or the facilities of another carrier are not reasonably available to law enforcement for implementing the interception of communications or access to call-identifying information; and

compliance with the requirements of this subchapter is reasonably achievable through the application of available technology to the equipment, facility, or service at issue or would have been reasonably achievable if timely action had been taken.

Upon issuing an order enforcing this subchapter, the court shall specify a reasonable time and conditions for complying with its order, considering the good faith efforts to comply in a timely manner, any effect on the carrier’s, manufacturer’s, or service provider’s ability to continue to do business, the degree of culpability or delay in undertaking efforts to comply, and such other matters as justice may require.
An order enforcing this subchapter may not—
require a telecommunications carrier to meet the Government’s

children.tail: So in original. Probably should not be capitalized.
demand for interception of communications and acquisition of call-identifying information to any extent in excess of the capacity for which the Attorney General has agreed to reimburse such carrier;

children.text: require any telecommunications carrier to comply with assistance capability requirement

children.tail: So in original. Probably should be “requirements”.
if the Commission has determined (pursuant to
) that compliance is not reasonably achievable, unless the Attorney General has agreed (pursuant to
) to pay the costs described in

children.text: require a telecommunications carrier to modify, for the purpose of complying with the assistance capability requirements of

children.tail: , any equipment, facility, or service deployed on or before

children.text: the Attorney General has agreed to pay the telecommunications carrier for all reasonable costs directly associated with modifications necessary to bring the equipment, facility, or service into compliance with those requirements; or
the equipment, facility, or service has been replaced or significantly upgraded or otherwise undergoes major modification.
